question_title: Will the interest in “grants for reentry programs 2026” change between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-14 according to Google Trends?
summary: The search term "grants for reentry programs 2026" experienced a massive spike from 0 to 100 in the resolution date range, driven by the DOL $81M RESTART grant announcement on Feb 25. By March 3, it has decayed to 77. With 11 days remaining until March 14, standard post-spike decay for this niche government grant term (baseline = 0 for 2+ weeks prior) predicts values around 30-35 — far below the 74 threshold needed for "Doesn't change." The resolution space is highly asymmetric: Decreases covers 0-73 (74 points), while Doesn't change only covers 74-80 (7 points). Monte Carlo simulations with multiple scenarios (including 5% probability of a second spike and 5% sustained interest) yield ~96% probability of Decreases.

factors.description: Post-spike decay: The search term was at 0 for 2+ weeks before a news-driven spike to 100. By Mar 3 it's already decayed to 77. Standard news spike decay predicts ~30-35 by Mar 14, well below the 74 threshold for 'Doesn't change'.

High starting value (77) creates asymmetric resolution space: Decreases covers 0-73 (74 points), Doesn't change covers 74-80 (7 points), Increases covers 81-100 (20 points). The narrow DC band at elevated levels strongly favors Decreases.
Active grant application deadlines in late March/April (RESTART, OJJDP) could sustain some search interest, but unlikely at 74+ level for this specific niche query.
Possible second news spike (DOGE budget cuts, new grant announcements, political controversy about reentry programs) could temporarily boost interest.
Niche government grant term with very low baseline (0 for weeks before spike) - interest fundamentally lacks a sustained audience to keep searches elevated.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (95%)**

The search term "grants for reentry programs 2026" is in post-spike decay. Key findings:

- **Spike driver**: DOL announced $81M RESTART grants on Feb 25, driving interest from 0 to 100 (peak Feb 28)
- **Current state**: Already decayed to 77 on Mar 3 in the resolution date range
- **Resolution threshold**: Value on Mar 14 must stay within ±3 of 77 (i.e., 74-80) for "Doesn't change"
- **Decay projection**: Exponential decay modeling predicts ~30-35 by Mar 14, far below the 74 threshold
- **Baseline**: The term was at 0 for 2+ weeks before the spike — there is no sustained organic audience

The only paths to non-Decrease resolution would require a second major news spike or extraordinary sustained application-related interest at near-peak levels, both of which are very unlikely for this niche term.

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| Decreases | 0.95 |
| Increases | 0.03 |
| Doesn't change | 0.02 |

[(Metaculus) Will the interest in “grants for reentry programs 2026” change between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-14 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42414)
resolution_reason: The Google Trends value for "grants for reentry programs 2026" on 2026-03-14 is 0, compared to 74 on 2026-03-03. This represents a decrease of 74 points, which is far more than the 3-point threshold specified in the resolution criteria. The question resolves to "Decreases".
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=grants%20for%20reentry%20programs%202026&date=2026-02-12%202026-03-14](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=grants%20for%20reentry%20programs%202026&date=2026-02-12%202026-03-14) for 2026-03-14 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-03. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
